claims.text: Cross-domain reinforcement learning aims to improve data efficiency by leveraging samples from a source domain to facilitate learning in a similar target domain
claims.source_quote: Cross-domain reinforcement learning (CDRL) is meant to improve the data efficiency of RL by leveraging the data samples collected from a source domain to facilitate the learning in a similar target domain.

claims.text: Cross-domain transfer in reinforcement learning has two fundamental challenges: different state/action spaces and uncertain model transferability
claims.source_quote: Despite its potential, cross-domain transfer in RL is known to have two fundamental and intertwined challenges: (i) The source and target domains can have distinct state space or action space... (ii) The transferability of a source-domain model in RL is not easily identifiable a priori

claims.text: The authors propose cross-domain Bellman consistency as a method to measure model transferability between domains
claims.source_quote: we first introduce the notion of cross-domain Bellman consistency as a way to measure transferability of a source-domain model

claims.text: Q-Avatar combines Q functions from source and target domains using an adaptive hyperparameter-free weight function
claims.source_quote: we propose $Q$Avatar, which combines the Q functions from both the source and target domains with an adaptive hyperparameter-free weight function

claims.text: Q-Avatar achieves reliable transfer by effectively leveraging source-domain Q functions for knowledge transfer to the target domain
claims.source_quote: we characterize the convergence behavior of $Q$Avatar and show that $Q$Avatar achieves reliable transfer in the sense that it effectively leverages a source-domain Q function for knowledge transfer to the target domain

claims.text: Q-Avatar demonstrated favorable transferability across locomotion and robot arm manipulation reinforcement learning benchmark tasks
claims.source_quote: Through experiments, we demonstrate that $Q$Avatar achieves favorable transferability across various RL benchmark tasks, including locomotion and robot arm manipulation
